title: Out-of-order Execution
summary: Explains out-of-order execution: instructions begin or finish in a different order than written when no data dependency prevents it, eliminating pipeline stalls.
content_points: Instructions that are independent can begin and complete out of program order.
Purpose: keep pipeline stages busy while a stalled instruction waits.
Example: 'in al,278; add bl,al; mov edx,[ebp+8]' – third instruction has no dependency; may complete before first two.
Results are still committed in-order to preserve program semantics.
learning_objectives: Explain when out-of-order execution is applicable
Describe how in-order commit maintains correct program behaviour
